input.reasoning_trace:
STEP 1 — TAF vs GFS reconciliation:
The TAF issuance (2026-04-21T11:38Z) is NEWER than the GFS model run (2026-04-21T06:00Z), so the TAF prevails for blocks within its validity window (12Z Apr 21 – 18Z Apr 22).

Block 2026-04-21T12:00Z: TAF base from 12Z shows scattered/broken/overcast sky with showers in vicinity; GFS had rain likely. The 15Z group shifts to light showers rain, overcast. No thunderstorm signal in the 12–18Z window per TAF (the prob30 thunderstorm window is 19–23Z). GFS CAPE is very low (13 J/kg). Provisional keeps precipExpectation=likely, precipPhenomenon=rain, severeFlag=false — this aligns with TAF. TAF wind 12 kt → keep windKt min/max. No change needed from provisional here.

Block 2026-04-21T18:00Z: TAF Prob30 from 19–23Z includes thunderstorm rain, and the base/15Z groups show persistent rain. GFS CAPE still low (13 J/kg). The TAF introduces a thunderstorm probability (30%) in this window but CAPE is low. Thunderstorm signal is present but low-energy → not severe. Update precipPhenomenon from "rain" to "thunderstorm" for this block given TAF thunderstorm mention; severeFlag remains false (low CAPE, non-severe). gustOutlook: TAF doesn't specify gusts in later periods, but observation shows active gusts — keeping "occasional" from provisional.

STEP 2 — Current observation override (obs at 13:53Z):
Obs time 13:53Z falls within the 12:00Z block (covers 12:00–18:00Z). Observation shows:
- Wind 230° at 25 mph (~22 kt), gusting 47 mph (~41 kt). This is significant — 41 kt gusts are at tropical-storm-force gust level. Update windKt for 12Z block: min ~20 kt, max ~22 kt sustained; gusts to 41 kt → gustOutlook=frequent.
- Visibility 1.5 SM (~poor). Update visibilityExpectation=poor for 12Z block.
- Sky: Broken cumulonimbus at 2500 ft, overcast at 6000 ft → overcast.
- Weather: Heavy Thunderstorm Rain Hail — this is a SEVERE thunderstorm event (hail present, strong winds). Update precipPhenomenon=thunderstorm, severeFlag=TRUE for 12Z block.
- Temperature 66°F = ~18.9°C — within range.

The observation confirms an active severe thunderstorm with hail right now. The 12Z block is updated to reflect: thunderstorm, severeFlag=true, windKt min 20 max 22, gustOutlook=frequent, visibilityExpectation=poor.

The 18Z block: obs is at 13:53Z, which is just over 1 hour away from 15:00 local / 18:00Z boundary. Per rules, observation overrides hour X and X+1. 13:53Z is in the 12Z block; 18Z block is ~4 hours later and not directly overridden by the observation. However the TAF's Prob30 thunderstorm for 19–23Z and the ongoing severe event inform the 18Z block. Given the active severe storm and TAF thunderstorm signal in the 19–23Z window, I will update the 18Z block's precipPhenomenon to thunderstorm, but severeFlag remains false there (the storm activity may be tapering; Prob30 only, low CAPE overall). Wind for 18Z: TAF shows 100–110° at 11–12 kt in later periods — the storm will likely move through; revert to TAF wind guidance for 18Z block: windKt min 6 max 12 (retain provisional). visibilityExpectation: TAF shows 4SM Prob30 and 6SM base → reduced (not poor) for 18Z.

STEP 3 — Prior snapshot comparison:
Prior snapshot had the 12Z block as: overcast, rain likely, severeFlag=false, windKt 7–12, gustOutlook=none, visibilityExpectation=reduced.

NEW vs PRIOR for 12Z block:
- precipPhenomenon: rain → thunderstorm (CHANGE)
- severeFlag: false → TRUE (CHANGE — SAFETY CRITICAL: severe thunderstorm with hail)
- windKt: 7–12 → 20–22 (massive increase, gusts to 41 kt — tropical-storm-force gusts)
- gustOutlook: none → frequent
- visibilityExpectation: reduced → poor

This is unambiguously safety-critical news. The prior forecast promised rain; active severe thunderstorms with hail, near-tropical-storm-force gusts, and very poor visibility are now occurring. This absolutely warrants an unscheduled send.

For the 18Z block: prior had severeFlag=false, rain. Updated to thunderstorm (non-severe) — plans-affecting but paired with the safety-critical 12Z change, so included.

Final snapshot retains the two blocks from the provisional (06Z block from prior has expired; only 12Z and 18Z blocks are in the provisional). Temperatures converted: 66°F observed = ~18.9°C; GFS high 68°F = ~20°C, low 60°F = ~15.6°C.

Wind conversions for email: 20–22 kt sustained = ~23–25 mph; gusts 41 kt = ~47 mph. 18Z block 6–12 kt = ~7–14 mph.
input.email_body:
Spring, TX
Observed: Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 8:53 AM local
Unscheduled update — see note below
What's changed: A severe thunderstorm is now underway with confirmed hail, very low visibility, and powerful wind gusts — conditions are significantly more dangerous than the rain forecast in your last update. Thunderstorm activity may continue into this evening.
Current Conditions
at Houston, David Wayne Hooks Memorial Airport
Sky Low broken cumulonimbus, overcast above
Visibility 1.5 miles
Wind Southwest at 25 mph, gusting to 47 mph
Weather Heavy thunderstorm with rain and hail
Temperature 66°F
Relative Humidity 88%
Pressure 29.92 inHg
Forecast for Spring, TX
Date Temperatures Wind Conditions
Tue Apr 21
Noon–6 PM		 High: 68°F
Low: 61°F		 23–25 mph
Gusts to 47 mph		 Severe thunderstorms with hail and very poor visibility likely.
Tue Apr 21
6 PM–Midnight		 High: 68°F
Low: 63°F		 7–14 mph
Occasional gusts		 Thunderstorms likely to continue through the evening.
Wed Apr 22 High: 84°F
Low: 63°F		 Up to 12 mph Overcast with isolated showers; storms possible, energy elevated.
Thu Apr 23 High: 82°F
Low: 67°F		 Up to 14 mph Mostly overcast; isolated showers possible with elevated storm energy.
In summary: A severe thunderstorm is impacting the area right now, bringing hail, very poor visibility, and near-tropical-storm-force wind gusts — please stay indoors and away from windows. Conditions are expected to gradually ease this evening, though the storm risk remains elevated through Wednesday.
